item.title: Owning Your Memory: Hardened Arenas, Channels, and Structural OOP in Pure C99
item.description: Assumes working familiarity with C, a basic tolerance for explicit memory ownership, and an interest in what happens when you take the phrase “auditable in one sitting” literally. Language migrations do not eliminate memory-safety problems; they move them. The CVEs follow the toolchain: the allocator, the package registry, the compiler plugin ecosystem, the language runtime. Each new dependency chain is a new attack surface with a different owner and a different disclosure timeline.
